author: Pxlern
text: @jtrevorchapman Good write up. Personally I never trust back tests. I always build and test in real time. Yes it takes much longer, but I can learn as the market progresses. Also the majority of back test data out there isn’t good enough for my systems.

author: jtrevorchapman
text: @Pxlern Yeah I agree, backtests on binary markets are mostly worthless. I still backtest to get a general idea, knowing that going live will result in far worse results.

author: round_trippa
text: @jtrevorchapman Do you ever have dramas with your redemption? I seem to constanly have problems trying to redeem back to my wallet. I feel like i create a new fix a couple times a day. Anything bulletproof?

author: jtrevorchapman
text: @round_trippa That’s never affected me. Since pol is relatively cheap I pay 1.5x the average transaction. But I do first ping to verify the oracle had settled the market. I’ll ask opus to write out how my redemption process works. I’ve basically taken the same process from bot to bot since my

author: jujulele5w
text: @jtrevorchapman Impressive. Your approach seems like, entry by momentum likelihood -> try lock (hedge position seems quite far from the entry though) -> halt if success, exit function if fails? Am I correct? If so, how could you decide it is time for the reversal then try exit?

author: nokolo_
text: @jtrevorchapman Where can I read more about the strategy?
